zh.lifeSummary: 當Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie於0982年3月21日出生在法國布列塔尼雷斯時，她的父親Conan Ier de Rennes duc de Bretagne38歲，母親Ermengarde d'Anjou20歲。她於1000年與Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie在法國諾門迪結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1017年6月16日在法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利盧享年35歲，埋葬在法國諾門迪艾波內艾。

nameMeanings.name: Judith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘Jewess’ or ‘woman from Judea’, borne by a Jewish heroine whose story is recorded in the Book of Judith in the Apocrypha. Judith is portrayed as a beautiful widow who delivers her people from the invading Assyrians by gaining the confidence of their commander, Holofernes, and cutting off his head while he is asleep; without their commander, the Assyrians are duly routed. This has been a perennially popular Jewish name. In the English-speaking world it was taken up in the 16th century, having been in occasional use among Gentiles before this: for example, it was borne by a niece of William the Conqueror. It enjoyed great popularity between the 1940s and the 1960s. Today's notable bearers include the American novelist  Judith Krantz  (  b. 1928 ) and the Scottish composer  Judith Weir  (  b. 1954 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie was born on 21 March 0982, in Rennes, Brittany, France, her father, Conan Ier de Rennes duc de Bretagne, was 38 and her mother, Ermengarde d'Anjou, was 20. She married Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie in 1000, in Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 16 June 1017, in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Upper Normandy, France, at the age of 35, and was buried in Bernay, Eure, Normandy, France.

Elle a épousé Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie en 1000, à Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 juin 1017, à Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, France, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterrée à Bernay, Eure, Normandie, France.

März 0982 in Rennes, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Conan Ier de Rennes duc de Bretagne 38 und ihre Mutter Ermengarde d'Anjou 20. Sie hat 1000 in Normandie, Frankreich Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. Juni 1017 im Alter von 35 Jahren in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Bernay, Eure, Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie(이)가 0982년 3월 21일 프랑스 브레타그니 렌스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Conan Ier de Rennes duc de Bretagne(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ermengarde d'Anjou(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1000년 프랑스 노먼디에서 Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1017년 6월 16일 프랑스 하테-노먼디 세이인-마이팀 루언에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노먼디 우르 버나이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie nasceu em 21 de março de 0982, em Rennes, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Conan Ier de Rennes duc de Bretagne, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Ermengarde d'Anjou, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie em 1000, em Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de junho de 1017, em Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, França, com 35 anos, e foi sepultada em Bernay, Eure, Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie родилась 21 марта 0982 г. в местности реннесь, бретагн, Франция, её отцу по имени Conan Ier de Rennes duc de Bretagne было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Ermengarde d'Anjou, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie в 1000 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 16 июня 1017 г. в местности роуен, сине-маритим, хауте-норманди, Франция в возрасте 35 лет и была похоронена в местности бирнеи, эур, норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie nació el 21 de marzo de 0982, en Rennes, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Conan Ier de Rennes duc de Bretagne, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Ermengarde d'Anjou, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie en 1000, en Normandie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 16 de junio de 1017, en Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 35 años, y fue sepultada en Bernay, Eure, Normandie, Francia.
